title: Onondaga County expands hotel room tax to include short-term rentals
summary: Onondaga County expanded its hotel room occupancy tax to include short-term rental properties booked through platforms like Airbnb and VRBO, effective immediately. The measure requires a county short-term rental registry and is projected to generate $1 million in annual revenue. Tax collection will be automated through booking platforms for most owners, with non-platform users required to remit taxes manually.
department_refs: Office of the County Fiscal Officer

political_signal.description: County prioritizing tax revenue from emerging short-term rental market while establishing regulatory infrastructure through registry; framed as equity measure to level playing field with traditional hotels.

processing_notes: Dollar amount mechanical extraction incomplete: corrected to $1 million annually
Onondaga County action, not direct City of Syracuse jurisdiction—may impact Syracuse constituents and tax base
Registry requirement creates data governance opportunity for CIO office regarding short-term rental inventory tracking
Software platform integration with third-party booking sites relevant to digital services infrastructure
